reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 7.1% and 20-day momentum is 10.7%.
Trend quality is supported by 60% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 6.4% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.5%, downside volatility near 0.9%, and a recent max drawdown of 2.7%.
Volume confirmation is -1.2% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 5.2% versus SPY over 5 days and 5.0% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.88, confidence 0.84, and 1.0 weighted articles across 2 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.88, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 2 sources, 1.0 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.96, average source quality 0.83, confidence 0.84.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.02).
Overall model score is 0.249 with high confidence.

news_evidence.title: Honeywell International Inc. filed 8-K: 8-K
news_evidence.llm_reason: An SEC Form 8-K for Honeywell is inherently company-specific and can contain market-moving disclosures. However, no filing contents are provided here, so the potential impact over the next 1-10 trading days is uncertain and cannot be scored as high.

news_evidence.title: Honeywell International Nears June 29 Split With 'All Green Lights,' Reaffirms Outlook
news_evidence.llm_reason: Direct management commentary: CFO said the planned separation is on track for June 29 and reiterated the outlook. This is timely, company-specific, and likely to influence investor expectations and flows in the next 1-10 trading days (positive bias).
